THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - MOTION TO QUASH IS DENIED. THERE IS NO REQUIREMENT UNDER SECTION 474 THAT A PLAINTIFF EXERCISE REASONABLE DILIGENCE IN DISCOVERING THE TRUE IDENTITY OF FICTITIOUS DEFENDANTS. 184 CA 3RD 376. THIS IS SO EVEN IF IGNORANCE DUE TO PLAINTIFF'S NEGLIGENCE. 183 CA 3RD 352. DEFENDANT MUST PROVIDE EVIDENCE THAT PLAINTIFF WAS ACTUALLY AWARE OF DEFENDANT'S NAME AT THE TIME OF FILING. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS APPLIES SEPARATELY FOR EACH ASBESTOS-RELATED DISEASE, 83 CA 4TH 1444. STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS WOULD NOT RUN AS TO ASBESTOSIS IF THAT WAS FIRST DIAGNOSED IN DECEMBER '03. COUNSEL WILL CONFER ON PROPOSED ORDER. JUDGE: JOHN K. STEWART, REPORTER: SUSAN CROZIER, CSR #4761

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jul-10-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT KYLE RAILWAYS, INC.'S RENEWED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
APPEARANCES: Kimberly J. Chu, Esq. of Brayton Purcell, LLP 898-1555 for pltf & opposing party; Tim Killelea, Esq. of Gordon & Rees LLP 986-5900 for deft & moving party.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - denied. Plaintiff implicitly asserts that it is only seeking to hold Kyle liable for conduct of California Western Railroad. Kyle did not shift the burden regarding plaintiff's ignorance that Kyle is the successor of California Western Railroad at the time he filed the complaint. Plaintiff to submit proposed form of order. (302/HEK) JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-30-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
James Nevin of Brayton Purcell (898.1555) for plaintiff and responding party
Michael Plocki of Gordon & Rees (986.5900) for defendant and moving party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT KYLE RAILWAYS, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Defendant failed to shift the burden of proof because defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiff knew that "Kyle Railways, Inc" was his employer as contrasted with "California Western Railroad" when the complaint was filed. Plaintiff's deposition testimony is ambiguous as to when he knew defendant was his employer. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

MOTION To Quash Service Of Summons And Defendant Kyle Railways, Inc.S Demurrer To Plaintiff Geral Brooks Complaint And Amendment To Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-22-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT KYLE RAILWAYS, INC.'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS AND DEFENDANT KYLE RAILWAYS, INC.'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF GERALD BROOKS' COMPLAINT AND AMENDMENT TO COMPLAINT.
APPEARANCES: Oren P. Noah, Esq. of Brayton Purcell 898-1555 for pltf & opposing party; Michael J. Plocki, Esq. of Gordon & Rees LLP 986-5900 for deft & moving party (both submitting).
RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Motion to quash is denied. There is no requirement under section 474 that a plaintiff exercise reasonable diligence in discovering the true identity of fictitious defendants. 184 CA 3rd 376. This is so even if ignorance due to plaintiff's negligence. 183 CA 3rd 352. Defendant must provide evidence that plaintiff was actually aware of defendant's name at the time of filing. The Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend. The statute of limitations applies separately for each asbestos-related disease, 83 CA 4th 1444. Statute of limitations would not run as to asbestosis if that was first diagnosed in December '03. Counsel will confer on proposed order. (302/JKS/xSN) JUDGE: JOHN K. STEWART, REPORTER: SUSAN CROZIER, CSR #4761
